description: Tier 1b fresh-decls task set for the Bridge Experiment (docs/research/BRIDGE-EXPERIMENT.md). Theorems added to Mathlib master AFTER the Brain decl snapshot, with every retrieval index pinned older => held out from all arms (including the Wikibrain). TheoremGraph recipe at larger n.

held_out.guarantee: every kept decl name is ABSENT from both the pinned decl universe (statement_formal.csv, 388105 decls) and the Brain nodes (brain/data/nodes.jsonl), and was added to Mathlib master strictly after the pin date.

determinacy_second_annotator.note: Independent second determinacy annotation (BRIDGE-ISSUES.md #11), judged from informal_statement alone before seeing gold_formal/determinate. Verdicts stored per-row as det2/det2_reason; determinate/determinacy_reason (annotator 1) untouched. The PRIMARY analysis set is the 74 rows where BOTH annotators call the informal statement determinate. Cohen's kappa ~= 0.20 (slight-to-fair): the annotators apply different criteria more than they apply the same criterion inconsistently -- annotator 1 flags sibling near-duplicates (the informal matches several lemmas in a family), annotator 2 flags semantic underdetermination (missing domain/codomain types, garbled glosses, ambiguous quantifiers) and one byte-identical NL pair (034==036). Of 21 disagreements: 9 are det1-indeterminate/det2-determinate (annotator 1's family/near-duplicate calls), 12 are det1-determinate/det2-indeterminate (annotator 2's type/semantic calls).

grading_note: gold_formal ends `:= sorry` and is the exact Mathlib master statement. It uses standard Mathlib section-variable/open style, so it typechecks standalone only after prepending gold_context (the enclosing variable/open/universe lines, given in scope order) under gold_header; a superset header `import Mathlib` also works. Grading = BEq+/equivalence vs gold (typecheck is never success), per the design.
determinacy_note: Determinacy is now a TWO-annotator screen (design calls for two independent formalizers). counts.determinate (86) is annotator 1's first-pass; annotator 2 is in determinacy_second_annotator (83 determinate). The determinate fraction is high because candidates were pre-filtered to docstringed, self-contained statements and bespoke-helper/unrecoverable-hypothesis decls were dropped outright rather than flagged. The design's contamination-proof primary set = the 74 rows both annotators call determinate; report the excluded fraction both ways (14% single-annotator, 26% either-annotator-excludes).
